steps.title: Question type
steps.content: Reading comprehension — logical completion of a hypothesis. Key facts: N. pudica's pitchers are underground (unique among carnivorous plants); it grows on dry ridges where few other plants and animals live on the surface.

steps.title: Reasoning
steps.content: If surface life is sparse but prey may exist underground, an underground trap would capture more prey than a surface trap could in that habitat — an adaptive advantage explanation.

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'one of many undiscovered species': the passage gives no basis to predict additional undiscovered species; doesn't follow from the surface-vs-underground observation. ✗
  • Choice B — 'formed pitchers early to absorb moisture': pitchers are described as prey traps, not moisture absorbers; nothing about developmental timing is given. ✗
  • Choice C — 'buried by animals foraging': the researchers "unearthed" the plants and note few animals on the surface; suggests the plants grow underground, not that animals bury them. Contradicts the text. ✗
That matches choice D — D is correct.
